user_prompt: This module will handle two main goals

You know how we can send a "pick up" message over HFP? But we don't really know if we will be picking up or hanging up? The UI will have a button that issues that message, but the icon for that button will be in one of two states: pick-up (a phone with an up arrow), and pick-up-or-hang (a phone with both an up and down arrow). A state machine will be implemented, and the state will change according to the call state messages coming from the phone. The icon will change according to the state machine.
and in my design, only `PhoneUiState::IncomingCall` will show the "pick-up" icon, while all other states show the bi-directional icon.
wire this in to BtManager's event handler
Only the state needs to be exposed by a getter.

The next goal: obtain as much data as possible about the caller, like caller ID, phone number, contact name, etc. Have a linked list of strings, and just add to this list when new information arrives (check for duplicates though, we don't want duplicates). The list is cleared the moment a bluetooth connection is established. The first entry should be the Bluetooth host's reported friendly name, if available. The getter can get the number of entries, and also can get any one of the list elements by an index. Call it something like `callerInfo` like `getCallerInfoCnt` and `getCallerInfoAt`.
agent_reply: - Keeps caller info as a linked list of deduplicated strings.
- Clears caller info when a Bluetooth connection reaches SLC connected.
- Adds the known Bluetooth host friendly name first when available.
- Queries current-call metadata when calls ring/setup/become active, so `CLCC` has a chance to populate.
I left the existing auto-answer behavior unchanged for now: incoming call setup and ring still call `answer_incoming_call_once(...)`.
Build passed. `src/version.c` was regenerated by the build.

user_prompt: move `trim_start` `trimmed_length` and `clone_trimmed_string` to `lib\UtilFuncs\utilfuncs.cpp`, mark `clone_trimmed_string` with a comment with a warning saying that the cloned string needs to be free'ed
agent_reply: - Added the warning comment that `clone_trimmed_string()` returns malloc’d memory that must be `free`’d.
- Updated `CallManager.cpp` to include `utilfuncs.h` and use the shared helper.
Build passed. `src/version.c` was regenerated again by the build.

user_prompt: When a recording is finished inside AudioFileRecorder::stopRecording , use CallManager to generate a chunk of text to write into the file with AudioFileRecorder::queueMetaText if the recording was a call. If it was a memo, figure out what RecordingView says is the memo-type, use AudioFileRecorder::queueMetaText to make it say “memo-type:<x>”. If possible, rename the file so that the first letter captures what the memo type was.
agent_reply: The recorder now queues final metadata from inside `AudioFileRecorder::stopRecording`: calls get a `CallManager`-formatted text block, and memos get `memo-type:<x>`. The recording view now reports memo-type changes to the recorder, and stopped memo files are renamed after close so the first filename letter matches the final memo type when possible. Promoted memo-to-Bluetooth recordings are marked as call recordings.

Note: `pio run -e thefly` still fails because that env does not define `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL`; that is unrelated to this change. `src/version.c` was already dirty before I started, and the build script regenerated its hash.

commit_message: wired up the meta text stuff for recorded files
